text.title: ruby-activerecord Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ruby-activerecord as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: includes vs eager_load confusion: User.includes(:posts).where('posts.flagged = true') without references(:posts).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: includes heuristically picks strategy; adding a where on the joined table silently switches to eager_load (one big LEFT OUTER JOIN). Use references(:posts) or split the query explicitly to make the join type visible.

failure-mode.description: update_all used on audited or paper-trailed models.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: update_all bypasses callbacks silently — counter_cache, paper_trail, and audit logs break without any error. Use update! per record or explicitly disable callbacks with a comment explaining why.

failure-mode.description: Hidden default_scope causes missing rows; agent writes User.where(...) and gets fewer results than expected.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force agents to grep for default_scope before any query change. Agents debugging "missing data" rarely reach for withoutGlobalScopes.

failure-mode.description: save vs save! — agent uses save and does not check return; record silently fails validation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Standardize on save! + rescue at the boundary, or always check .persisted? after save.

failure-mode.description: Memoization on a relation: @users ||= User.where(...) returns a relation that re-queries on each .each.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Memoize .to_a not the relation; otherwise memoization has no effect.

failure-mode.description: Strong migrations bypass: add_index :users, :email blocks writes for minutes on large tables.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use algorithm: :concurrently and disable_ddl_transaction! — strong_migrations gem enforces this in development.

failure-mode.description: first without order is non-deterministic in Postgres under concurrent inserts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always specify an explicit order: User.order(:created_at).first — or use find(id) when you have the ID.

failure-mode.description: N+1 by default. Lazy associations are AR's killer trap. Without includes / preload / eager_load, agents copy patterns straight into prod regressions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use Bullet and Prosopite gems to gate N+1 detections in CI. Run every test with both enabled.

failure-mode.description: Callback chains explode. before_save → after_commit → after_create_commit-driven logic re-fires on tests, on fixture loads, and at the wrong transactional boundary.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keep callbacks simple and idempotent. Test callbacks separately in isolation. Use after_create_commit not after_create for email/notification side effects.

failure-mode.description: Caching invalidation gaps. AR cache_key_with_version invalidates on updated_at; agents forget touch: true on belongs_to, parent caches go stale.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always add touch: true on belongs_to associations that have counter_cache. Test cache behavior explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Query object growing into a god class. Agent adds a method per use case; the query object grows to 30 methods covering one-off filters.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cap at ~10 chainable methods; if more needed, split by use case into multiple query objects. Review query objects for single responsibility.

failure-mode.description: Polymorphic belongs_to typos. Wrong as: argument silently writes wrong type strings; integrity is not enforced.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a before_validation to enforce the type from a constant list. Test polymorphic associations in specs.

failure-mode.description: Test DB ≠ prod DB. Agents test on sqlite, ship to Postgres; case sensitivity, JSON column behavior, and array types diverge.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force Postgres in CI from day 1. Run migrations in CI and on a real Postgres instance before merging.

failure-mode.description: Schema drift across branches. Two agents add migrations with the same timestamp on parallel branches; merge produces non-deterministic order.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use bin/rails db:migrate:status in CI to catch timestamp collisions. Enforce migration naming to prevent conflicts.

failure-mode.description: destroy cascade through dependent: :destroy can fan out to thousands of records.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force agents to find_each + explicit destroy when chains are deep, or switch to soft delete + background cleanup job.
